title: What is Asukas heritage
wikitext:
[]: What is Asuka's heritage?             Quote Post by Rinjii » Fri Jan 04, 2008 7:26 pm I know she's of German descent and, judging by her as well as her biological mother's last name, of Japanese as well, but isn't she also mixed with American or something (I read this somewhere but am not sure if it's true)? And from which parent does she get each individual ethnicity from? The fact that her mother, father, stepmother, AND (I presume) grandmother could speak fluent Japanese in whatever parts of the show they were featured in only makes the matter more confusing, although I understand this was most likely done just for convenience.. y'know, to keep in line with the show's (original) regional language.

[]:             Quote Post by BEsERk EVA01 » Fri Jan 04, 2008 7:29 pm http://www.evageeks.org/FGP/Asuka_Langley_Soryu This should answer most of your questions. Enjoy.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Fri Jan 04, 2008 8:04 pm Wow...Bee-Zerk pwned. He said something that was right.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Fri Jan 04, 2008 8:17 pm The answer is that we're kind of confused on this but according to Reichu, Eva Chronicle says it more clearly: Asuka's mother Kyoko was half-Japanese, half-German (hence Asuka and Kyoko's Japanese names)....her father, a Mr. "Langley" was "American" The common belief is that Asuka is 3/4 German, when actually we just know she's 1/4 Japanese and at least 1/4 German, but her father's ethnicity was never specified, though I do believe he was caucasian (judging from the name "Langley" I didn't think he was asian, etc.).....at any rate the idea is that Asuka LOOKS Caucasian (so when people hold up an image of a Japanese girl cosplaying Asuka and go "this is what a live-action Asuka should look like" they're incorrect)...and Asuka has lived her entire life in Germany, speaks German as her native language, and apparently considers herself "German"...though through her father she technically holds dual-citizenship with the USA. At any rate, I think it odd that they don't say what her father's ethnicity is, but the point is "1/4 Japanese, 3/4...."Caucasian", appears Caucasian, considers herself "German"".

[]:             Quote Post by Rinjii » Fri Jan 04, 2008 8:28 pm Yeah, I'm going with the mother being part-Japanese, part-German, and the father being American theory. It makes the most sense, and this animeboards archive thread I just found a few minutes ago only echoes the theory. Thanks for the help, guys. :)

[]:             Quote Post by BEsERk EVA01 » Fri Jan 04, 2008 8:40 pm You're welcome, however, next time, with questions like these, it is better to Private Message someone because, sooner or later, a mod will come by and say something like, "It isn't worth a thread." :)

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Jan 04, 2008 8:44 pm Sources don't really say Asuka's dad was American as such. They say (and these are sources dating back to the beginning) that she's quarter German, quarter Japanese, and has American "nationality"/is an American citizen. The Langley was an American ship, so for all intents and purposes Daddy is an American. Going by the name, all one can really deduce is Anglo descent. Anyway, here, I'll just post this thing again. @Beserk: Mods would recommend the search function, actually.

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Fri Jan 04, 2008 8:48 pm Wow... Kyoko looks to have quite a beak there.

[]:             Quote Post by BEsERk EVA01 » Fri Jan 04, 2008 8:51 pm Sorry, ma'am. I just heard that you'd prefer PM than a whole thread, actually. Asuka does actually look kinda German-Japanese-American when you look at it, but she does actually look more American; because of the animator's intent or of the genetic "looks" I'm not entirely sure.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Jan 04, 2008 8:57 pm BEsERk EVA01 wrote: Asuka does actually look kinda like a generic anime chick lacking anything to distinguish her aside from hair style, color palette, and clothes/accessories when you think about it

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Fri Jan 04, 2008 9:05 pm Reichu wrote: BEsERk EVA01 wrote: Asuka does actually look kinda like a generic anime chick lacking anything to distinguish her aside from hair style, color palette, and clothes/accessories when you think about it

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Jan 04, 2008 9:09 pm Sachiel_13 wrote: Hey, since no American is fully American unless they're natives, couldn't that create a whole bunch of possibilities for Asuka Heritage?

[]:             Quote Post by BEsERk EVA01 » Fri Jan 04, 2008 9:21 pm Yeah, but for all we know, Asuka could be part-Alien, part-Evangelion, part-Angel or part-man. But, the 3 main cultures in Asuka's Heritage is fine.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Fri Jan 04, 2008 9:25 pm BEsERk EVA01 wrote: Yeah, but for all we know, Asuka could be part-Alien, part-Evangelion, part-Angel or part-man.

[]:             Quote Post by BEsERk EVA01 » Fri Jan 04, 2008 9:41 pm Sarcasm. Let's get back on topic, please. :cry:

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Fri Jan 04, 2008 9:44 pm On the topic of heritages, is everybody else on the show 100% Japanese?

[]:             Quote Post by BEsERk EVA01 » Fri Jan 04, 2008 9:50 pm Yes, I think so. Unless you count Asuka's father and mother.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Jan 04, 2008 10:34 pm Sachiel_13 wrote: On the topic of heritages, is everybody else on the show 100% Japanese?

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Fri Jan 04, 2008 10:36 pm BEsERk EVA01 wrote: Yeah, but for all we know, Asuka could be part-Alien, part-Evangelion, part-Angel, part-man or part-Predator.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Jan 05, 2008 4:32 am For further reading look up the Siebold Society, founded by Philipp Franz von Siebold (1796-1866) and chaired at one time by Dr. Constantin von Brandenstein-Zeppelin (a plausible candidate to be Asuka's maternal grandfather).
